title: alcea striata
intro: Alcea striata, also known as the prairie rose mallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Alcea striata include soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
